challenges.title: String Slicer
challenges.description: Given the string 'Learning Python', extract the word 'Python' using slicing and store it in a variable called 'word'.
challenges.starter_code:
text = 'Learning Python'
# Extract 'Python' from text using slicing
word = # your code here
hints: text[9:] will work since Python goes to the end

challenges.title: List Builder
challenges.description: Create a list called 'fruits' containing 'apple', 'banana', 'cherry'. Then add 'date' to the end using a list method. Finally, create 'count' storing the length of the list.
challenges.starter_code:
# Create the fruits list
fruits = # your code here

# Add 'date' to the end

# Store the length in count
count = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert isinstance(fruits, list), 'fruits should be a list'
assert fruits == ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry', 'date'], f'Expected ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "date"], got {fruits}'
assert count == 4, f'count should be 4, got {count}'
hints: Create a list with square brackets: ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']
Use len() to get the count

challenges.title: Dictionary Lookup
challenges.description: Create a dictionary called 'student' with keys 'name' (value: 'Alice'), 'age' (value: 21), and 'grade' (value: 'A'). Then create 'info' as a formatted string: 'Alice is 21 and got A'.
challenges.starter_code:
# Create the student dictionary
student = # your code here

# Create the info string
info = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert isinstance(student, dict), 'student should be a dictionary'
assert student['name'] == 'Alice', f"name should be 'Alice'"
assert student['age'] == 21, f'age should be 21'
assert student['grade'] == 'A', f"grade should be 'A'"
assert info == 'Alice is 21 and got A', f"info should be 'Alice is 21 and got A', got '{info}'"
hints: Use f-strings: f"{student['name']} is {student['age']} and got {student['grade']}"
